sections.heading: Why Breathing Exercises Work
sections.body: When you're stressed, your breathing becomes shallow and rapid — this signals danger to your brain, worsening anxiety. Controlled breathing does the opposite: it activates your parasympathetic nervous system ('rest and digest'), lowering heart rate, reducing cortisol, and calming your mind. It's the fastest evidence-based way to reduce acute stress — and it takes less than 2 minutes.

sections.heading: 4-7-8 Breathing (The Relaxation Breath)
sections.body: Developed by Dr. Andrew Weil, this technique is powerful for anxiety and sleep:

code.snippet:
4-7-8 BREATHING TECHNIQUE:

1. Place tongue tip behind upper front teeth
2. Exhale completely through mouth (whoosh sound)
3. Close mouth, inhale through nose for 4 counts
4. Hold breath for 7 counts
5. Exhale through mouth for 8 counts
6. Repeat 4 times

PRACTICE SCHEDULE:
• Week 1-2: 1 cycle, twice daily
• Week 3-4: 2 cycles, twice daily  
• Week 5+:  4 cycles, twice daily

WHEN TO USE:
• Before bed (fall asleep faster)
• During anxiety (calm racing thoughts)
• Before presentations (steady nerves)
• During code review stress (reset focus)

WHY IT WORKS:
The extended exhale (8 counts) stimulates the vagus nerve,
activating your parasympathetic nervous system.
The hold (7 counts) increases CO2 tolerance,
which reduces anxiety sensitivity.
code.explanation: The 4-7-8 ratio matters more than the absolute timing. As you practice, you can speed up (inhale 2 sec, hold 3.5 sec, exhale 4 sec) while keeping the same ratio.

sections.heading: Box Breathing (Navy SEAL Technique)
sections.body: Used by Navy SEALs to stay calm under fire, this is perfect for high-pressure work moments:

code.snippet:
BOX BREATHING:

Inhale 4 counts → Hold 4 counts → Exhale 4 counts → Hold 4 counts

    ┌──────────┐
    │ Inhale 4 │
    │          │
Hold│          │Hold
  4 │          │ 4
    │          │
    └──────────┘
    Exhale 4 counts

REPEAT: 4 times (64 seconds total)

USE CASES:
• During production incidents (stay calm while debugging)
• Before difficult conversations
• When overwhelmed by multiple tasks
• During on-call nights (manage adrenaline)

jargon.definition: The 'rest and digest' branch of your nervous system. Activated by slow breathing, it lowers heart rate, reduces cortisol, and promotes calm.
The longest cranial nerve, connecting brain to body. Stimulated by slow exhales, it triggers relaxation and reduces inflammation.
The primary stress hormone. Short bursts are helpful; chronic elevation causes anxiety, weight gain, and immune suppression.

summary: Breathing exercises activate your parasympathetic nervous system, reducing stress in under 2 minutes. The 4-7-8 technique (inhale 4, hold 7, exhale 8) is best for anxiety and sleep. Box breathing (4-4-4-4) is best for high-pressure work moments. Practice daily to build the habit.
